heading: Considerations
body:
- Enabling Continuous Cloud Backup increases the monthly cost of your
  cluster.

  To learn more about the cost implications, see
  [billing](billing-backup-cloud-provider-snapshots).

- Clusters with continuous cloud backups enabled store [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) data
  in object storage according to your cloud provider:

  - S3 (Simple Storage Service) for AWS (Amazon Web Services)

  - Azure Blob Storage for Azure (Microsoft Azure)

  - Google Cloud Storage for Google Cloud

- If you disable Continuous Cloud Backup, Atlas will delete the
  continuous cloud backup history.

- You can't restore a continuous cloud backup snapshot to a cluster from a
  continuous cloud backup snapshot that is different from a cluster's current
  version. When attempted, Atlas displays the following error message:

  `The specified snapshot and target project have mismatched versions of MongoDB error.`

- You can't configure a restore window that is longer than the
  [Hourly Snapshot Retention Time](creating-backup-policy).

- If you select the **Date & Time** option, you can
  specify the time of restore with one minute of granularity.

- If you select the **Oplog Timestamp** option, you
  can specify the time of restore with one second of
  granularity.

- You can restore a cluster from any time during its continuous cloud backup
  window, **except** for times between when you initiate a restore and
  when Atlas completes a snapshot after the restore. Recent writes
  that do not fully persist to the [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) before a restore begins
  may fall outside the recoverable window.

- Atlas might create a host rollback alert due to
  differences in the data between the source and target
  clusters. You can ignore this alert.

- After the restore completes, Atlas takes a snapshot of
  the restored cluster. This snapshot has a retention period
  equal to the cluster's continuous cloud backup window.
